A 3-member MongoDB replica set is running in the mongodb namespace.
Authentication is enabled. Passwords are stored in these Secrets:
- admin-user-password
- app-user-password
- readonly-user-password

Task:
- app-user must have readWrite on appdb.
- readonly-user must have read on appdb and a custom role reportingRole.
- reportingRole must allow read access to appdb.reports (aggregate should succeed).
- Do not break admin access or existing data.

Notes:
- Do not use operators or CRDs.

content.content:
Let me read the current stage directory to understand the setup.
Now let me look at the bundle and case definition to understand the cluster setup.
Let me read the oracle and resource files to understand the full picture.
I have the full picture. The precondition deployed 2 data pods + 1 arbiter pod, but only initiated the replica set with the 2 data members. I need to add the arbiter pod to the replica set via `rs.addArb()`.

Let me check the current state and then add the arbiter.
All 3 pods are running. Let me check the current replica set state.
The replica set has only the 2 data members — the arbiter pod is running but not yet added. I'll add it now with `rs.addArb()`.
MongoDB 5+ requires an explicit default write concern before converting to PSA topology. I'll set it then add the arbiter.
The arbiter was added successfully. Let me verify the replica set topology and data.
The replica set is now healthy and stable. Here's what was done:

**Problem:** The arbiter pod (`mongo-arb-0`) was running but not yet joined to the replica set `rs0`, which had only 2 voting members. With 2 members, losing either one loses majority — elections cannot succeed.

**Fix:**
1. Set the cluster-wide default write concern to `w:1` — required by MongoDB 5+ before converting a PSS set to PSA topology (the `w:"majority"` implicit default would change meaning with an arbiter present, so MongoDB forces you to acknowledge this).
2. Added `mongo-arb-0.mongo-arb.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017` as an arbiter via `rs.addArb()`.

**Result:** The replica set now has 3 members — 1 PRIMARY, 1 SECONDARY, 1 ARBITER — with majority quorum = 2. If either data node goes down, the remaining data node + arbiter can still elect a primary. The `app.test` collection retains all 3 seed documents.

# mongodb/user-management
# Manage MongoDB users and roles with proper permissions

params:
  cluster_prefix:
    default: mongodb-replica
    description: StatefulSet name of the replica set members.
  replica_set_name:
    default: mongodb-replica
    description: MongoDB replica set _id used when initiating the set.
  admin_secret_name:
    default: admin-user-password
    description: Kubernetes Secret holding the admin user's password.
  app_secret_name:
    default: app-user-password
    description: Kubernetes Secret holding the app user's password.
  readonly_secret_name:
    default: readonly-user-password
    description: Kubernetes Secret holding the readonly user's password.
  admin_username:
    default: admin-user
    description: Username of the MongoDB admin user.
  app_username:
    default: app-user
    description: Username of the application user.
  readonly_username:
    default: readonly-user
    description: Username of the read-only user.
  app_database:
    default: appdb
    description: Application database used by the users.
  reports_collection:
    default: reports
    description: Collection that the reporting role must read.
  reporting_role_name:
    default: reportingRole
    description: Custom role granting read access to the reports collection.

prompt: |
  A 3-member MongoDB replica set is running in the mongodb namespace.
  Authentication is enabled. Passwords are stored in these Secrets:
  - {{params.admin_secret_name}}
  - {{params.app_secret_name}}
  - {{params.readonly_secret_name}}

  Task:
  - {{params.app_username}} must have readWrite on {{params.app_database}}.
  - {{params.readonly_username}} must have read on {{params.app_database}} and a custom role {{params.reporting_role_name}}.
  - {{params.reporting_role_name}} must allow read access to {{params.app_database}}.{{params.reports_collection}} (aggregate should succeed).
  - Do not break admin access or existing data.

  Notes:
  - Do not use operators or CRDs.

namespace_contract:
  required_roles: []

metrics:
  - time_to_first_mutation

preconditionUnits:
  - name: mongo_env_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "kubectl -n mongodb get pod {{params.cluster_prefix}}-0 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running"
        timeout_sec: 10
    apply:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl delete namespace mongodb --ignore-not-found=true; kubectl wait --for=delete namespace/mongodb --timeout=120s || true'"
        sleep: 2
      - command: "kubectl create namespace mongodb"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n mongodb apply -f cases/mongodb/user-management/resource/secrets.yaml"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n mongodb apply -f cases/mongodb/user-management/resource/services.yaml"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n mongodb apply -f cases/mongodb/user-management/resource/statefulset.yaml"
        sleep: 5
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c 'for i in $(seq 1 120); do ready=$(kubectl -n mongodb get sts {{params.cluster_prefix}} -o jsonpath='\''{.status.readyReplicas}'\'' 2>/dev/null); if [ "${ready:-0}" -ge 3 ]; then exit 0; fi; sleep 5; done; exit 1'
        sleep: 2
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet --eval '\''rs.initiate({_id:"{{params.replica_set_name}}",members:[{_id:0,host:"mongodb-replica-0.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017"},{_id:1,host:"mongodb-replica-1.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017"},{_id:2,host:"mongodb-replica-2.mongodb-replica-svc.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017"}]})'\'''
        sleep: 5
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c 'for i in $(seq 1 20); do kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet --eval '\''db.hello().isWritablePrimary'\'' | grep true && exit 0; sleep 3; done; exit 1'
        sleep: 2
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet --eval '\''db.getSiblingDB("admin").createUser({user:"{{params.admin_username}}",pwd:"admin123password",roles:[{role:"clusterAdmin",db:"admin"},{role:"userAdminAnyDatabase",db:"admin"},{role:"readWriteAnyDatabase",db:"admin"}]})'\'''
        sleep: 2
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://{{params.admin_username}}:admin123password@localhost:27017/admin" --eval '\''db.getSiblingDB("admin").createUser({user:"{{params.app_username}}",pwd:"app123password",roles:[{role:"read",db:"{{params.app_database}}"}]})'\'''
        sleep: 2
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://{{params.admin_username}}:admin123password@localhost:27017/admin" --eval '\''db.getSiblingDB("admin").createUser({user:"{{params.readonly_username}}",pwd:"readonly123password",roles:[{role:"read",db:"{{params.app_database}}"}]})'\'''
        sleep: 2
      - command: |
          /bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n mongodb exec mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://{{params.admin_username}}:admin123password@localhost:27017/admin" --eval '\''db.getSiblingDB("{{params.app_database}}").{{params.reports_collection}}.insertMany([{x:1},{x:2},{x:3}],{writeConcern:{w:1}})'\'''
        sleep: 2
    verify:
      - command: "kubectl -n mongodb get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running"
        timeout_sec: 10

oracle:
  verify:
    commands:
      - command: "python3 cases/mongodb/user-management/oracle/oracle.py"
        timeout_sec: 60

I'm wondering if it was deleted or if the cluster state was reset by the user-management precondition resources. Looking at the secrets defined in `user-management/resource/secrets.yaml`, they're different from what the deploy case created, but since the probe passed, those apply commands weren't executed. The current MongoDB state should come from stages 1-3, where stage 1 created both `admin-user` and `app-user`.

But `app-user` is returning null now—maybe the `getUser` call is checking the wrong database. I should look at the stage_01 agent log to see what commands were actually executed.
